summary: Tested backend REST APIs (11 pytest tests, all passing) and React dashboard UI end-to-end via Playwright. Backend endpoints /api/stats, /api/posts, /api/posts/{id}, /api/media/{id}, /api/channels and DELETE /api/posts/{id} behave as specified (correct status codes, no Mongo _id in responses, sorted-desc list, proper 404s). Dashboard loads, shows 4 stat cards with correct labels/values, seeded 'Тестовая статья' visible in the table, row click opens detail Sheet with title/media/blocks/telegraph link, copy-link buttons write correct URL to clipboard, refresh-btn reloads data, open-bot-link points to t.me. Seeded post was NOT deleted.

issues: Radix DialogContent console warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby={undefined}'. Accessibility only — add SheetDescription or aria-describedby to SheetContent.

action_items: (Optional a11y) Add <SheetDescription> to the post-detail Sheet to silence Radix warning.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py get_stats aggregates media_count field from posts; if a post document ever lacks that field, $sum treats it as 0 — OK, but confirm telegram_bot always sets media_count on inserts.
DELETE /api/posts silently swallows GridFS delete errors (bucket.delete inside try/except: pass). Acceptable, but logging the exception would help diagnose orphaned media.
server.py uses deprecated FastAPI on_event('startup'/'shutdown'); consider migrating to lifespan context manager (non-blocking).
get_channels serializes settings docs manually but relies on Mongo _id being the user_id (custom key) — works, but fragile if schema changes. Prefer explicit projection.
get_media returns Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000 even for potentially private media — acceptable for public dashboard use case but worth noting.
seed_data_creation: None. Used existing seeded post id=f707d0a3-2995-474e-9fe2-2defd72776fa (title 'Тестовая статья') with media 6a4a3d9760a2983cd674e5d0. DELETE tested only on non-existent id, seeded post preserved.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend endpoints verified with pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_api.py. Playwright UI checked stats, posts table, detail sheet (open by clicking title cell — clicking directly on the whole row may race with animation; wait ~1.5-2s after click), copy buttons (mock navigator.clipboard.writeText to inspect), refresh-btn, open-bot-link. Telegram bot flow was intentionally NOT exercised (aiogram long-poll). Seeded post must remain intact for future dashboard tests.
